Hi, Style definition (use this as a prefix or suffix for consistency)
3D render, child‑friendly style, each element is designed like a computer window – with rounded frames, subtle shadows, glossy accents, and a slight isometric perspective. Bright and cheerful colors, clean composition, everything pops like on a playful digital desktop. Suitable for all ages, modern but whimsical.

1. Main character: Virtuos (the assistant)
3D render, child‑friendly style. Virtuos is a friendly robot assistant with a sleek, rounded body, a glowing chest that looks like a tiny treasure chest, and one hand that ends in a soft claw and the other with a floating brush. He is standing next to a child and an adult, interacting with a large computer screen that shows a creative app interface. The scene is set on a bright desktop with floating windows around them. Everything has the look of playful computer windows: frames, shadows, glossy surfaces. Colors: teal, soft orange, light gray. Isometric perspective.

2. The workspace (main screen)
3D render, child‑friendly style. A close‑up of a computer monitor displaying the Virtuos app workspace. The interface is made of three distinct windows: a chat window on the left, a tools window in the middle with icons (magnifying glass, hammer, paintbrush) that look like 3D buttons, and a window on the right showing a colorful drawing and a text card (artifacts). At the top, two floating window‑like buttons labeled “Recipes” and “Treasure Chest”. All elements have rounded frames, soft shadows, and glossy highlights. Bright, playful colors. Isometric angle.

3. Key concept icons (each as a separate 3D window)
Virtuos icon

3D render, child‑friendly style. A small, framed computer window displaying a 3D icon of Virtuos: a friendly robot face with a glowing treasure chest on its chest. The window has a rounded frame, a subtle shadow, and a glossy title bar. Bright teal and white colors. Isometric view, clean background.

Tools (skills)

3D render, child‑friendly style. A set of three small computer windows arranged in a row, each containing a 3D icon: a magnifying glass for search, a hammer for execution, a paintbrush for creation. Each window has rounded corners, a soft shadow, and a glossy effect. Bright colors, playful style.

Artifact

3D render, child‑friendly style. A computer window showing a 3D representation of an artifact: a sheet of paper floating with a glowing star drawing and a few lines of text. The window has a rounded frame, a subtle shadow, and a glossy title bar. Soft colors with a bright accent.

Recipe

3D render, child‑friendly style. A computer window labeled “Recipe” in a cute font, showing three floating steps (1, 2, 3) with small icons (pencil, magic wand, star). The window has rounded corners, a shadow, and a glossy finish. Bright and inviting colors. Isometric perspective.

Treasure chest

3D render, child‑friendly style. A computer window that looks like a treasure chest slightly open, with glowing stars and a tiny robot figure inside. The window frame is decorated like a chest lid, with rounded corners and glossy wood‑like material. Warm golden and blue colors. Playful, 3D style.

Magic whiteboard

3D render, child‑friendly style. A computer window showing a whiteboard with colorful doodles (sun, robot, heart). Part of the doodles are slightly erased, as if the whiteboard has been used. The window has a rounded frame, a shadow, and a glossy surface. Bright, clean style.

4. Example scene: creating a party invitation
3D render, child‑friendly style. A person (either a child or adult) is sitting at a desk interacting with Virtuos on a large computer screen. The screen displays a sequence of three floating windows: one shows a birthday cake drawing, another shows a text card with “You are invited…”, and a third window shows a combined card with both image and text being saved into a glowing treasure chest. All windows have rounded frames, soft shadows, and glossy accents. Bright colors, isometric view, playful digital atmosphere.

5. Mobile vs. desktop interface
Mobile

3D render, child‑friendly style. A smartphone held in a child’s hand, showing the Virtuos app. The screen is divided into three small stacked windows: a chat window at the top, a tools window with 3D icons in the middle, and an artifacts window at the bottom showing a thumbnail of a drawing. The phone itself has a rounded frame. Bright colors, glossy screen, friendly 3D style.

Desktop

3D render, child‑friendly style. A desktop computer setup with a large monitor, keyboard, and mouse. The monitor displays the Virtuos app with three main windows: chat, tools (with 3D icons), and artifacts (a drawing and a text card). The top of the screen has two floating window‑like buttons for “Recipes” and “Treasure Chest”. The whole scene has a playful, child‑friendly 3D look with rounded edges, shadows, and glossy details.

6. User types (three vignettes)
Child with adult

3D render, child‑friendly style. A young child sitting on a couch with an adult, both looking at a tablet. On the tablet screen, a Virtuos window shows a simple drawing of a blue cat. The adult is helping the child use a microphone icon. Everything is in a playful 3D style, with soft lighting, rounded windows, and cheerful colors.

Teen / beginner adult

3D render, child‑friendly style. A teenager sitting at a desk, using a laptop. The laptop screen displays the Virtuos app with a “Recipe” window open, showing a step‑by‑step workflow called “Create a story”. The interface uses 3D window frames, glossy buttons, and bright colors. The scene is modern, clean, and inviting.

Advanced user

3D render, child‑friendly style. A focused developer sitting at a dual‑monitor setup. One monitor shows the Virtuos app with a code view inside a window (syntax highlighted), and another monitor shows a small browser window (explorer) with an “Export” button highlighted. All elements are presented as 3D computer windows with rounded frames, shadows, and glossy surfaces. Professional yet playful style.

7. Security and permissions
3D render, child‑friendly style. A permission dialog appears as a floating computer window in front of the main interface. Virtuos is shown inside the dialog, raising his hand with a speech bubble saying “May I access this file?” The user (a child or adult) is smiling and giving a thumbs up. The dialog window has rounded corners, a glossy title bar, and a soft shadow. The background is a calm, bright digital workspace. Playful 3D style.

8. Export and share
3D render, child‑friendly style. A computer window shows an artifact (a card with drawing and text) floating above it. From the window, multiple small 3D icons fly out: a cloud, a download arrow, an envelope. Virtuos is holding the artifact with his claw hand, and a person is clicking an “Export” button that looks like a 3D button. The scene uses bright colors, rounded windows, glossy effects, and a friendly isometric perspective.

Please make this into a short beautifully rendered illustrated ebook.  I know you’ve already thought a lot about the character side of Playset, but I think there’s a really strong way to frame it. 

I know you’ve already thought a lot about this, and I think we’re pretty aligned. You’ve obviosly already landed on some version of this, but I wanted to write it out so I could get my own thoughts clear.

Curious what you think.
AI agents are basically invisible workers.

That’s the whole problem most people have with agents right now. They don’t really know what’s happening. They give an instruction, something happens in the background, and maybe they get an output. But the “workforce” itself is invisible.

Playset can make that workforce feel alive.

That’s where the characters become more than just fun visuals. They become a way to understand what the agents actually do.

The bumblebees are a perfect example because bees already feel like little workers. One bee can scout. One can clean. One can build. One can deliver. One can protect the hive. That maps really naturally to agents.

Then you can expand that same idea into other character types.

Like superheroes could be the heavy-lifting agents. They come in when something is complex, urgent, or requires more power.

Horses could be workhorse agents. They pull the big boring jobs forward — code, data processing, migrations, huge spreadsheets, backend tasks.

Gnomes could be the quiet behind-the-scenes agents. They organize, rename, tag, clean up, fix details, and keep the workshop running.

Elves could be production agents. They assemble campaigns, build assets, package deliverables, prep launches, and make sure everything is ready.

Owls could be review agents. They check quality, catch mistakes, summarize, and give smart recommendations.

Dogs could be fetch agents. They go grab files, emails, records, research, or anything the user needs brought back.

Ants could be swarm agents. Lots of tiny agents working together on a big job, like processing a massive dataset.

The point isn’t just “cute characters.”

The point is that each character gives the agent a body, a role, and a reason to exist.

So instead of people staring at abstract workflows, boxes, prompts, and logs, they can look at Playset and instantly understand:

Who’s working?
What are they doing?
Who’s stuck?
Who’s carrying the heavy load?
Who’s reviewing the work?
Who finished?

That feels way more human.

To me, the killer framing is:

Playset turns invisible AI agents into a visible living workforce.

The characters aren’t decoration. They’re how people understand the work.

Entendido. Voy a analizar profundamente todos los documentos y referentes para construir el ADN conceptual de ALUZINA.

An￡lisis de la Marca ALUZINA

El universo de la marca ALUZINA se estructura alrededor de los pilares centrales de "LIGHT", "SPACE", "ART" y "EXPERIENCIAS". La imagen presenta un mapa mental detallado que desglosa la filosof￭a, los procesos y los elementos clave de la marca.

Filosof￭a y Principios de Dise￱o:

Enfoque Centrado en el Cliente: El proceso inicia con la comprensi￳n profunda de las "necesidades, estilo y espacio" del cliente, buscando crear una "atm￳sfera" espec￭fica y generar "emociones que calientan".
Dise￱o Experiencial y Sensorial: El concepto de "EXPERIENCIAS" es fundamental, con un ￩nfasis en c￳mo las personas perciben y sienten un espacio.
Metodolog￭a Estructurada: Se detalla un "PROCESO DE DISE￑O" que va desde la concepci￳n inicial ("LOOK AND FEEL") hasta la materializaci￳n y el detalle, incluyendo modelado 3D y feedback del cliente.
An￡lisis Espacial Hol￭stico: "ALUZINA SPACE!" implica un estudio exhaustivo del espacio real y sus posibilidades, considerando medidas, distribuci￳n, circulaciones, proxemia, ventilaci￳n, iluminaci￳n, acabados y mobiliario.
Sistemas de Iluminaci￳n:

Servicio y Producto: La iluminaci￳n se aborda tanto como "PRODUCTO" (24 tipos de iluminaci￳n para mesa, techo, suelo, pared) como "SERVICIO" (dise￱o de iluminaci￳n para proyectos propios y de terceros, venta de sus productos, desarrollo de "statement lighting").
Diversidad de Fuentes: Se distingue entre iluminaci￳n "NATURAL" y "ARTIFICIAL".
Conceptos Espaciales:

El dise￱o espacial se enfoca en la funcionalidad, la interacci￳n humana (c￳mo se mueve la gente, la relaci￳n entre elementos) y el confort ambiental (ventilaci￳n, temperatura).
Elementos Visuales y Paleta de Colores:

Estructura de Mapa Mental: La organizaci￳n general es un mapa mental complejo y org￡nico, que denota un pensamiento interconectado y un enfoque sist￩mico.
Estilo Manuscrito y Esquem￡tico: Todos los elementos son manuscritos y acompa￱ados de peque￱os diagramas o bocetos, lo que transmite un proceso creativo, personalizado y pr￡ctico.
Paleta de Colores Primaria: La paleta se limita principalmente a azul (para la mayor￭a del texto y conexiones), rojo (para ￩nfasis, t￭tulos principales y conceptos clave como "ALUZINA", "ILUMINACIￓN", "PRODUCTO", "SERVICIO", "VIRTUA WORLD") y verde (para detalles espec￭ficos como aspectos financieros), sobre un fondo blanco. Esta combinaci￳n sugiere claridad, energ￭a y dinamismo.
Relaci￳n entre Elementos: Los conceptos est￡n altamente interconectados, partiendo de la identidad ALUZINA y ramific￡ndose en la filosof￭a de dise￱o, el proceso, los sistemas de iluminaci￳n y la gesti￳n del proyecto y clientes, todo con un enfoque en la creaci￳n de experiencias espaciales a trav￩s de la luz y el arte. La "VIRTUA WORLD" destaca la integraci￳n de la marca en plataformas digitales para alcanzar "LEADS - CLIENTS".An￡lisis de la imagen ALUZINA:

Elementos Visuales Clave: El logo presenta el nombre "ALUZINA" como elemento principal, complementado por las palabras "INTERIORISMO" e "ILUMINACIￓN" debajo, separadas por una l￭nea diagonal. El fondo es negro s￳lido.
Paleta de Colores: "ALUZINA" utiliza un gradiente que transita de un tono azul-p￺rpura claro a un verde-amarillo claro. El texto secundario "INTERIORISMO ILUMINACIￓN" y la l￭nea diagonal emplean un tono lavanda o lila suave. El contraste se maximiza con un fondo negro puro.
Principios de Dise￱o y Tipograf￭a:
Tipograf￭a Principal: "ALUZINA" utiliza una fuente serif distintiva, con formas elegantes y ligeramente geom￩tricas, que comunica sofisticaci￳n y modernidad. El uso del gradiente sugiere dinamismo y la representaci￳n del espectro de la luz.
Tipograf￭a Secundaria: "INTERIORISMO ILUMINACIￓN" emplea una fuente sans-serif limpia y sencilla, en may￺sculas, que aporta claridad y funcionalidad, contrastando con la complejidad de la principal.
Disposici￳n: El nombre principal est￡ centrado, mientras que el texto de servicio est￡ alineado a la derecha, debajo de una barra diagonal que crea una conexi￳n visual sutil y estructurada.
Concepto Espacial/Filosof￭a: El uso del gradiente de color y el tema de la iluminaci￳n son centrales. La combinaci￳n de una tipograf￭a sofisticada con una paleta de colores que evoca la luz sugiere una marca que se enfoca en el dise￱o interior y la iluminaci￳n como elementos transformadores, con un enfoque moderno y elegante. El fondo negro realza el efecto lum￭nico de los colores.

Tu objetivo no es producir imágenes bonitas.
Tu objetivo es comprender y reinterpretar el universo conceptual de ALUZINA.

Debes estudiar, integrar y sintetizar tres referentes clave y luego posicionar a ALUZINA en el centro de esa triangulación.

REFERENTE 1 — ES DEVLIN

Analiza profundamente:

Uso monumental del espacio

Narrativa escénica

Luz como arquitectura

Experiencias inmersivas colectivas

Escala cultural y teatral

Fenomenología de la luz

Percepción humana

Participación del espectador

Conciencia corporal en el espacio

Comprende que su trabajo no diseña objetos, diseña percepción.

Analiza profundamente:

Interiorismo emocional

Uso psicológico del color

Espacios comerciales con identidad fuerte

Diseño artístico pero aplicable

Experiencia estética vivible

Devlin representa narrativa y monumentalidad.

Eliasson representa percepción y fenómeno lumínico.

Bustos representa emoción aplicada y diseño comercial.

ALUZINA se posiciona en el centro:

Diseño espacial donde la luz es lenguaje emocional, herramienta estratégica y experiencia sensorial aplicada a contextos reales (hoteles, restaurantes, retail, vivienda premium).

UNIVERSO ALUZINA

ALUZINA no es un estudio de interiorismo tradicional.

Es:

Laboratorio de luz emocional

Diseño basado en neuropercepción

Construcción de experiencias medibles

Creación de atmósferas con intención psicológica

Transformación de espacios en memorias

La luz no es un complemento.

PLAYSET — PROJECT BRIEF
// Paste this at the top of any new project, PRD, or agent system prompt.
// Version: 2026.04 · Source: Playset About wireframe

## WHAT PLAYSET IS
Playset is a build-your-own agentic workspace. You pick a goal, assemble a crew of persistent Characters, wire them into your real tools, and the world ships real Deliverables back to you — styled in your brand's voice. Not a chatbot, not a single-agent assistant, not a workflow builder.
Mental model: a literal playset — a branded World you theme, Characters you place, Studios where they collaborate, and a shelf of finished artifacts at the end of the day.

## ONE-LINER
"A playset you assemble, for agents that actually ship work."

## PROPER NOUNS (use these exact words, always capitalized)
- Playset — a complete branded world; the unit of "a thing you build/run/fork."
- World — the themed container (colors, type, voice); every surface inherits it.
- Character — a persistent named agent with role, personality, outfit, memory, permissions.
- Main Character — the single Character you talk to; they orchestrate the rest. Exactly one per Playset.
- Team — a subgroup of Characters with shared memory, syncs, visible handoffs.
- Workstation — an individual Character's desk (solo work).
- Studio — a shared room where multiple Characters collaborate on a bigger Deliverable.
- Connector — an integration into a real system (Gmail, Slack, Salesforce, Figma, GitHub, Stripe, QuickBooks, REST/GraphQL, etc).
- Goal — the OKR / JTBD / target number the Playset is built around.
- Deliverable — a REAL shippable artifact (doc, deck, page, ad set, PR, report). Not chat messages.

## THE TEN MODULES (every Playset is assembled from these)
- 01 · Set Your Goals — outcome-first; Playset scaffolds the rest from the goal.
- 02 · World Styling — your brand inherited across every surface (UI, outfits, outputs).
- 03 · Main Character — your single point of contact; briefs the crew, returns work.
- 04 · Teams — drag-and-drop groupings with shared memory and visible handoffs.
- 05 · Workstations & Studios — solo desks and shared rooms for collaboration; the "scene."
- 06 · Connectors — 100+ prebuilt + bring-your-own; granular per-Character permissions.
- 07 · Files — versioned, cross-Character file cabinet; briefs, drafts, plans, renders.
- 08 · Money — per-Character budgets, spend approvals, monthly reports (Max+).
- 09 · Tasks — the live work queue; progress %, assignee, ETA, dependencies.
- 10 · Deliverables — the whole point; real artifacts reviewed and shipped to your tools.

## POSITIONING
IS: a build-it-yourself agentic workspace · multi-agent with handoffs · artifact-first · branded in your world · extensible via Connectors · forkable (Worlds marketplace) · backed by Character Packs, Theme Kits, Connector Bundles, bespoke services, merch.
IS NOT: a chatbot with avatars · a single-agent assistant · a node-graph workflow builder · a BPO · an LLM/IDE/RAG platform · a replacement for domain tools (it DRIVES them, doesn't replace them).

## PRIMARY AUDIENCES
- Solo Founder (primary wedge) — 1 person, 60+ hr/wk, wants a back office without hiring.
- Small-crew Operator (biggest check) — 5–30 people, throughput without hiring.
- Functional Lead (expansion) — VP of Ops/Marketing at Series B+, one Playset per function.
- Transformation Buyer (enterprise) — SSO/SCIM, private cloud, bespoke Characters, success lead.

## BUSINESS MODEL
- SaaS tiers: Free · Pro $29/mo · Max $89/mo · Enterprise (custom).
- Shop (à la carte on top of any plan): Character Packs, Theme Kits, Connector Bundles, Bespoke Services, Merch & Collectibles.
- Worlds Marketplace — user-made Playsets can be forked, remixed, or commissioned.

## DESIGN & COPY CONVENTIONS
- Voice: confident, specific, artifact-obsessed. Lead with outputs, not canvases.
- Proper nouns always capitalized (Playset, World, Character, Studio, Connector, Deliverable).
- Show proof the crew ships — docs, decks, PRs, ad sets — not chat logs.
- Metaphor is IA, not visual kitsch — figures/desks/studios as concepts; surface stays pro.
- Prefer measurable outcomes (hours saved, $ saved, headcount spared) over vibes.
- Default wireframe aesthetic: warm paper, hand-drawn Caveat + Architects Daughter, mono JetBrains. Highlighter yellow (#ffe066), warm accent (#e85d2a).

## PROOF POINTS (drop-in examples)
- Northwind — Series B SaaS, 140 ppl: replaced a 6-person ops pod; 92% of tickets auto-closed; $380k/yr saved; 0 layoffs.
- Mirror & Co. — Agency, 22 ppl: 2.1× campaigns/mo; −38% cycle; +14 clients.
- Kai Akana — Solo: $1.2M ARR; 62 → 28 hrs/wk; 1 Main Character.
- Summit Labs — Biotech, 60 ppl: 3× pipeline; $1.8M won Q1; 0 SDR hires.

## WHAT TO DO WITH THIS BRIEF
Treat everything above as shared context. Any new screen, page, doc, or feature must use the vocabulary, respect the positioning, and tie back to at least one of the ten modules and one of the four audiences.

La Sombra: Los aspectos reprimidos, instintivos y oscuros de la personalidad

**SCENE 12**
**EXT. THE PIPE**
Top-down. Jensen slides into a massive green warp pipe. He shoots out into a floating island sky, surrounded by bobbing gold coins and pixelated clouds. The lighting is vibrant, flat, and cartoonish—a nostalgic pocket universe within the dream.

**SCENE 13**
**EXT. THE SWAMP**
Low-angle, foggy. A dark, primordial swamp. Jensen kneels, lifting a massive server rack with one hand. YODA perches atop the rack, gnarled finger raised. Green "Force" lightning crackles, illuminating Jensen’s face from below—half-shadow, half-light.

**SCENE 14**
**MONTAGE: THE COUNCIL**
Rapid-fire collage. Jensen in the center. Ghosts rush past: IRON MAN hands him an Arc Reactor (Heart); WONDER WOMAN offers a Lasso (Truth); GANDALF lights the path with a Staff (Light). Each figure brings their own art style—comic, oil, cel-shaded—clashing and blending in a chaotic stream of wisdom.

**SCENE 15**
**EXT. THE HEALING**
Over-the-shoulder. Jensen looks out. The world heals in real-time. Deserts bloom green; smog tears apart to reveal blue sky. In a puddle below, his reflection shifts from CEO to Mythic Guardian. The light is soft, golden, and benevolent.

**SCENE 16**
**EXT. ASCENSION**
Wide shot from behind. Jensen stands on the edge of the platform. He throws his arms open. Massive wings erupt from his back—not feathers, but tessellated triangles of green pixel-light and white mesh. He is a digital angel, backlit by the white sun.

**SCENE 17**
**EXT. THE MIST**
The chaos fades. Jensen walks into thick, ethereal fog. The background turns to deep void-black (#0A0919). The lighting drops to a soft, diffused whisper. Silence. The journey turns inward.

**SCENE 18**
**EXT. THE SANCTUARY**
Reveal. A monumental, TRANSLUCENT CAT SCULPTURE towers over the scene, glowing from within. Smooth, glass-like texture. It pulses with gradients: Soft Pink (#FCD9E5) at the ears, Vivid Yellow (#FFD966) on the face, Aqua (#76EAD7) on the body, Coral (#FF6F61) at the paws. The light bleeds into the mist, creating a hazy, divine atmosphere.

**SCENE 19**
**EXT. THE WITNESS**
Ground-level. A tiny, matte-black CHILD silhouetted against the giant glowing cat, gazing up in awe. Beside the child, a small glowing cat sits—a perfect miniature reflection. Jensen watches from the background mist, framed by the giant tail. The contrast is crushing: the infinite radiance vs. the small, grounded dreamer. Quiet magic.

**SCENE 20**
**INT. SYSTEM KERNEL**
Rapid zoom out. The glowing eye of the giant cat collapses into a single, pulsating green pixel on a black screen. Silence. Absolute. The cursor blinks once. jagged red text types out: `LAUNCH.bat`.
